taxonomy: Sambirania plicata is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae. It belongs to the genus Sambirania, which is a monotypic genus consisting of only one species, S. plicata. Sambirania plicata is native to the tropical forests of Madagascar, an island nation in the Indian Ocean. The plant is classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree, characterized by its dark green, leathery leaves and showy white flowers. Further classification of Sambirania plicata can be broken down into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species, with the full classification being: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Magnoliophyta, Class: Magnoliopsida, Order: Malpighiales, Family: Clusiaceae, Genus: Sambirania, Species: S. plicata.
